title: Modeling Cycles, Units, Error, and Uncertainty · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Evaluate demand and capacity for a public program using incomplete measurements. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a validated demand-capacity model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Turn stakeholder needs into answerable model questions, quantities, constraints, and validation criteria.
Track units, scales, significant digits, and uncertainty through multi-stage calculations.
Estimate how measurement and rounding error affect a computed result.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 19 units, changes by −6 units, then by +2 units. Calculate the final quantity. Use the result to decide Model Validation for a validated demand-capacity model.
steps.why: The Model Validation result for a validated demand-capacity model is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 19 − 6 + 2; compare with the starting scale. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate a model against held-out cases, limits, units, and stakeholder behavior.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Before calculating Model Validation, predict the sign and approximate magnitude for a validated demand-capacity model. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then reconcile it with that prediction and A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate a model against held-out cases, limits, units, and stakeholder behavior.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 19 − 6 + 2; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Validate a model against held-out cases, limits, units, and stakeholder behavior.
